deprecations.replacement: Input facade was removed in Laravel 6. Inject Illuminate\Http\Request and use $request->input(...) / $request->all() / $request->only([...]).
dispatchNow() was deprecated in Laravel 8 and removed in Laravel 9. Use dispatchSync(): MyJob::dispatchSync($payload).
Closure-based Mail::send([...], $data, $closure) is legacy and not queueable. Generate a Mailable: php artisan make:mail InvoicePaid, then Mail::to($user)->queue(new InvoicePaid($invoice)).
Global unguard() disables mass-assignment protection for the entire request. Set per-model $fillable allowlists and pass $request->validated() to Model::create().
Closure-based Bus::chain([fn() => ...]) is deprecated. Use invokable Job classes: Bus::chain([new ProcessOrder($order), new SendInvoice($order)])->dispatch().
Avoid the JsonResponse->getData(true) anti-pattern in production code — it serializes then immediately deserializes the response. Build the JsonResource / array directly and return it once.
Str::random() is non-cryptographic. For secrets / passwords / tokens use Str::password() (Laravel 10.20+) or random_bytes(32) + bin2hex().
Password::min(N)->requireUppercase()/->requireNumeric()/->requireSymbols() were renamed in Laravel 10 to ->mixedCase(), ->numbers(), ->symbols(), ->letters(). Use the new fluent names.

Verify Route::resource(...)->only([...]) / ->except([...]) action names match Laravel's current resource map. Custom resource verbs that worked pre-Laravel 8 may need ->parameters([...]) or ->shallow() updates in Laravel 10+.
The --dev flag for `artisan serve` was removed. The dev server now picks up .env automatically; use APP_ENV=local php artisan serve if needed.
Closure-based Queue::after(fn() => ...) registration was removed in Laravel 11. Create an event listener class for Illuminate\Queue\Events\JobProcessed and register it in EventServiceProvider.

Laravel 11 removed App\Http\Kernel, App\Console\Kernel, and App\Exceptions\Handler. Configure middleware, scheduling, and exception handling fluently in bootstrap/app.php (->withMiddleware, ->withExceptions, ->withRouting). Schedule lives in routes/console.php.
